DRAFT_WARNING: UNRATIFIED DRAFT. Not committed to MPC-Spec. Defines cross-impl wire (rust-mpc consumes it) and therefore requires cross-impl byte-lock coordination (Python primary + Rust cross-validator + rust-mpc agreement) before promotion to MPC-Spec conformance/test-vectors/07-brc31-auth.json. Signature fields are placeholders pending a canonical bsv-rs create_signature run with pinned identity keys. See 07-brc31-auth.vector.README.md.
encoding: bsv-rs auth wire (bsv_rs::auth). General-message payload = HttpRequest::to_payload / HttpResponse::to_payload. Handshake signing per AuthMessage::signing_data + get_key_id.

payload_format.request: [request_id: 32B][method: varint+str][path: varint+str | -1][search: varint+str | -1][headers: varint count + (varint+key, varint+val) pairs][body: varint+bytes | -1]
payload_format.response: [request_id: 32B][status: varint][headers: varint count + (varint+key, varint+val) pairs][body: varint+bytes | -1]
payload_format.varint: value<253 -> 1 byte; <0x10000 -> 0xFD + u16 LE; <0x1_0000_0000 -> 0xFE + u32 LE; else 0xFF + u64 LE; value -1 (empty/missing) -> 0xFF repeated 9 times
payload_format.signed_headers: Only x-bsv-* (excluding x-bsv-auth-*), authorization, and content-type are carried in the payload, sorted bytewise by key. The x-bsv-auth-* transport headers (version, identity-key, nonce, your-nonce, signature, request-id) are NOT part of the signed payload.
payload_format.general_signing_data: = the General-message payload bytes verbatim (no version byte prefix)
payload_format.handshake_signing_data: InitialResponse signs yourNonce(initiator, base64-decoded) || initialNonce(responder, base64-decoded). key_id = "{yourNonce} {initialNonce}". InitialRequest is unsigned (starts the handshake).

test_only_keys.WARNING: Pinned test material. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION.

PENDING_DERIVATION.note: These identity pubkeys and all signature_* fields below MUST be filled by a canonical bsv-rs run (PublicKey::from_private_key + Peer::sign_message via create_signature under protocol "auth message signature", SecurityLevel::Counterparty, the listed key_id, counterparty = Other(peer identity)). They are left as placeholders so no fabricated bytes get treated as byte-locked.

vectors.name: brc31-vector-A-handshake-initial-response
vectors.description: (a) Valid InitialRequest -> InitialResponse handshake. Initiator sends an unsigned InitialRequest carrying its session nonce (initialNonce). Responder replies with an InitialResponse signed over yourNonce||initialNonce at SecurityLevel::Counterparty. MUST verify and establish a session.

initial_request.note: InitialRequest is not signed (AuthMessage::signing_data returns empty for InitialRequest).

vectors.name: brc31-vector-B-general-request
vectors.description: (b) Valid General-message request payload + signature. A POST /sign/init request body, encoded via HttpRequest::to_payload, signed under the established session. signing_data == the payload itself. MUST verify.

vectors.name: brc31-vector-C-replay-reused-nonce
vectors.description: (c) Replay: a second request reuses the same per-request nonce on the same session. The (session_nonce = your_nonce, request_nonce = nonce) pair was already consumed. §07.1 MUST reject with HTTP 401. The check runs AFTER signature verification, so the signature itself is valid here — only the replay-nonce consumption fails.

replayed_pair.note: your_nonce = server (responder) session nonce; request_nonce = the fresh per-request nonce, reused verbatim from vector B's first send.

vectors.name: brc31-vector-D-wrong-identity
vectors.description: (d) Wrong identity: the request's x-bsv-auth-identity-key header does not match the identity the session signature was bound to (§07 identity-binding), OR the General-message signature does not verify against the claimed identity key. MUST reject with HTTP 401.

tamper.claimed_identity_hex: <a different valid secp256k1 pubkey, not the session identity>

expected.reason: §07 identity-binding: header identity != session identity / signature does not verify against claimed key

vectors.name: brc31-vector-E-malformed
vectors.description: (e) Malformed: a signed request missing required nonce headers (x-bsv-auth-nonce / x-bsv-auth-your-nonce) OR a truncated payload that fails to decode via HttpRequest::from_payload. MUST reject (protocol violation -> 401), NOT silently skip the replay/identity defenses, and NOT 500.

expected.note: explicitly 401 not 500 — see bsv-mpc commit a3e660d

cross_check.what_is_NOT_yet_locked: all signature_der_hex fields, both identity pubkeys — require a canonical bsv-rs create_signature run; see README
